ideas.intro: Ideas for projects, activities, books, games, and more for students with a high level of interest.

items.content:
[ ] Check out [this video](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=AJBBVQrRwqo) for a deeper look at the history of cartography.
[ ] View the Activity Section on page 9-10 of _Mapping the World with Art. _You can make a navigational quadrant and use it at night to find Polaris.
[ ] View a [video](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=OUYTG8cGtRI) on the difference between digital and paper maps

[ ] Watch these videos about the different continents you're studying: [Americas](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=NfM2lZq63hc&list=PLvJNSf-7NfrMjdMvjxg0eWJnV3yDPN_zn&index=5), [Asia](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=cInBgbH7UYw&list=PLvJNSf-7NfrMjdMvjxg0eWJnV3yDPN_zn&index=2), and [Europe](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=cJt5Yi48oQ0).
[ ] [Watch this video](https://youtu.be/khGviBLW-ho?si=X7OIw1S_rKg8s6Vb): Exploring the Sea Floor of the Gulf of St. Lawrence. 
[ ] [Learn more](https://youtu.be/66_uXl3dSx4?si=rvfwWGuqp-LneU0r) about the history of Greenland. 
[ ] Try these [classic Canadian recipes](https://canadianfoodfocus.org/in-your-kitchen/classic-canadian-dishes/) from the provinces you are studying.

[ ] [Watch this video ](https://youtu.be/51MqSCZ7si8?si=hKYWCj7ZsFTUUqiJ)from the Canadian Geographic Challenge National Final and see how many questions you can answer. 
[ ] Create a geography trivia game based on what you've learned so far this year. Challenge your family or class to see how many questions they can answer correctly. 
[ ] [Watch this video](https://youtu.be/M6TtQTsVlTY?si=JEJ-XfWveHucq2SP) on Nunavut: Canada's Unbelievable Ancient Landscapes
